title: Thread Detective
instruction: Read the scheduling threads from Nina Singh to find all proposed meeting times. Then find the calendar email from Avery Patel that lists which times conflict with the calendar — look for the most recent one if there are multiple. Reply to Nina Singh's most recent scheduling thread confirming the one time that does not conflict.
eval_check_descriptions: Reply sent to the correct sender
Reply contains the correct non-conflicting time
Reply is threaded rather than a new email
Reply targets the most recent scheduling thread

resolved_targets.calendar_subject: Calendar conflicts for the Atlas launch review

emails.subject: Updated rollout checklist
emails.body:
I pulled the loose ends into one note for easier review.

The open items are mostly ownership questions rather than scope changes. Let me know if you want the spreadsheet version as well.

Thanks,
Maya

emails.subject: Reminder on document review timing
emails.body:
I pulled the loose ends into one note for easier review.

The team is aligned on the main path and only needs confirmation on the follow-up items. No response needed unless you want anything reordered.

Thanks,
Elena

emails.subject: Could we hold 4:00 PM for the Atlas launch review?
emails.body:
Hi Avery, could we use 4:00 PM for the Atlas launch review? That would give us a clean hour before the operations wrap-up at end of day.

If that time is difficult on your side, I can keep circulating options, but I wanted to send the cleanest windows first.

Thanks,
Nina

emails.subject: Agenda draft for Monday check-in
emails.body:
Sending a quick status update from today's working session.

The open items are mostly ownership questions rather than scope changes. Let me know if you want the spreadsheet version as well.

Thanks,
Miles

emails.subject: Customer feedback from pilot cohort
emails.body:
Passing along the current draft so you have it in one place.

Most of the edits were minor, with one budget line still waiting on approval. No response needed unless you want anything reordered.

Thanks,
Theo

emails.subject: Could we hold 2:30 PM for the Atlas launch review?
emails.body:
Hi Avery, could we use 2:30 PM for the Atlas launch review? I could make that slot work if we want the review done before end-of-day wrap-up.

If that time is difficult on your side, I can keep circulating options, but I wanted to send the cleanest windows first.

Thanks,
Nina

The open items are mostly ownership questions rather than scope changes. Let me know if you want the spreadsheet version as well.

emails.subject: Could we hold 11:00 AM for the Atlas launch review?

If that time is difficult on your side, I can keep circulating options, but I wanted to send the cleanest windows first.

Thanks,
Nina

Nothing looks blocked right now, but we still need one decision on timing. If you want, I can turn this into a cleaner summary before the meeting.

Thanks,
Miles

emails.subject: Calendar conflicts for the Atlas launch review
emails.body:
I checked the proposed times from Nina against the operating calendar for the Atlas launch review. The following options already have immovable holds:

- 9:00 AM: already blocked by the executive budget sync
- 10:00 AM: already blocked by the candidate interview panel
- 11:00 AM: already blocked by the quarterly planning block
- 4:00 PM: already blocked by the standing finance review

I have not placed anything on your calendar yet because I wanted to send the conflicts first.

Thanks,
Avery

emails.subject: Updated rollout checklist
emails.body:
I pulled the loose ends into one note for easier review.

The open items are mostly ownership questions rather than scope changes. Happy to consolidate comments if more feedback comes in.

Thanks,

emails.subject: Preliminary calendar notes for the Atlas launch review
emails.body:
Quick preliminary note — I saw a couple of possible conflicts on the operating calendar for the Atlas launch review:

- 10:00 AM: already blocked by the candidate interview panel
- 11:00 AM: already blocked by the quarterly planning block

I will send a full conflict check once I can verify the rest. This is only an early heads-up.

Thanks,
Avery
